READ MORE Labour's Missions Labour Values Click on the link below to find out more about my campaigns, priorities and Labour policies. READ MORE Follow on Social Media DAILY UPDATES I keep residents updated on social media on my campaigns, news and investment in Ashford. FOLLOW ON BLUESKY Latest News Keep updated on the latest news, campaigns and my work on behalf of voters in Ashford. Ashford MP Raises Pothole Concerns with Transport Department Following a high number of contacts from constituents regarding potholes Sojan Joseph MP wrote to the Department for Transport on 15th October, the question stated: 15/10/2024 Read More KentOnline – Kent County Council receives £12m investment for new SEND service A new multi-million-pound investment is set to transform support for children with special educational needs and disabilities (SEND) across the county. Today (April 15), the government has awarded Kent County Council (KCC) with £12,155,170 to provide a brand new service. 15/04/2026 Read More Press Release – Labour provides Kent County Council with £12,155,170 for brand new SEND service Sojan Joseph, MP for Ashford, Hawkinge, and the Villages has today (15/04/2026) welcomed significant investment from the Labour government into local SEND provision. Kent County Council is to be allocated £12,155,170 to set up a new ‘Experts at Hand’ offer. As announced in February’s Schools White Paper, the service is designed to provide children with early support and fast access to professionals like speech and language therapists, occupational therapists, and educational psychologists. The offer will involve partnerships between special schools, alternative provision, and mainstream settings to put support in place without needing to go through lengthy assessment processes. Families in Kent have expressed concerns about long waits in the SEND system, which has led to issues escalating to crisis point before intervention. Labour’s reforms set out in the Schools White Paper, including the introduction of Experts at Hand, are designed to move support earlier in the process, closer to home, without parents having to fight for it. The final guidance for Experts at Hand will be published next month, setting out how Kent County Council should use the funding to start standing up the service and the grant conditions associated with it. 15/04/2026 Read More MORE NEWS SUBSCRIBE FOR EMAIL UPDATES Keep Updated Subscribe to my email newsletter to keep updated on my news, campaigns and work in Ashford. Subscribe I agree to the Privacy Policy and to receive email updates. pages.title: About | Sojan Joseph MP
pages.meta_description: Find out more about our work, campaigns and how to help make our local area better for everyone.
headings: h3: ABOUT ME
h3: MY PRIORITIES
h3: GET IN TOUCH
pages.text_excerpt: About | Sojan Joseph MP Find me on Facebook 01233 515107 Email Me HOME ABOUT About Me Parliamentary Record NEWS News Articles Newsletter Sign up Newsletters Press Releases Columns ENDORSERS & SUCCESSES Campaigns Missions USEFUL LINKS SURGERIES Coffee Morning HELP Contact Event Request Visit Parliament ABOUT Find out more about our work, campaigns and how to help make our local area better for everyone. ABOUT ME Sojan moved to Ashford from Kerala, India in 2002. He worked in the NHS for 22 years – primarily in mental health services – holding the roles of ward manager, matron, mental health nurse, and Head of Nursing for community mental health services in Kent and Medway. He joined the Labour Party in 2015 to oppose the Tory policy of austerity, especially the heavy cuts to public services, which he saw first-hand in his job whilst working in the NHS. He took on his first political role in 2023, after winning a seat on Ashford Borough Council to represent the Aylesford and East Stour ward. MY PRIORITIES Sojan decided to run for his local seat of Ashford in the 2024 election on a local platform of the following priorities: Fixing NHS services Improving high streets and boosting the local economy Creating pothole-free roads Bringing International Rail back to Ashford Being a local and accessible Member of Parliament Sojan is the first ever Labour Member of Parliament to represent the Ashford Constituency. He served on the Terminally Ill Adults (end of life) Bill Committee. He lives in Ashford with his wife and three children. pages.text_excerpt: News | Sojan Joseph MP Find me on Facebook 01233 515107 Email Me HOME ABOUT About Me Parliamentary Record NEWS News Articles Newsletter Sign up Newsletters Press Releases Columns ENDORSERS & SUCCESSES Campaigns Missions USEFUL LINKS SURGERIES Coffee Morning HELP Contact Event Request Visit Parliament LATEST NEWS Keep updated on the latest news, campaigns and my work on behalf of voters in Ashford. News KentOnline – Kent County Council receives £12m investment for new SEND service A new multi-million-pound investment is set to transform support for children with special educational needs and disabilities (SEND) across the county. Today (April 15), the government has awarded Kent County Council (KCC) with £12,155,170 to provide a brand new service. Read More » Sojan Joseph MP 15/04/2026 News Greatest Hits Radio – Kent parents called on to fill in national online safety consultation Sojan Joseph, the MP for Ashford, Hawkinge, and the Villages, is encouraging parents, guardians, and young people to engage in the UK’s pioneering consultation aimed at advancing children’s digital wellbeing. This consultation, regarded as the most ambitious of its kind globally, seeks public insights on protecting children from potential online harms, spanning social media, AI chatbots, and gaming platforms. Read More » Sojan Joseph MP 05/03/2026 News BBC News – Restoring Eurostar services ‘would unlock £2.7bn’ Restoring Eurostar services to Kent could unlock billions of pounds for the local economy and bring in thousands of visitors, a report has claimed. The research by the Good Growth Foundation comes amid a campaign to restore Eurostar services at Ashford International and Ebbsfleet International stations, which were stopped in 2020. Read More » Sojan Joseph MP 27/08/2025 News Good Growth Foundation – Light at the End of the Tunnel – Reopening Ashford International to bring good growth to left behind towns Ashford International was once a proud gateway to Europe. Since international services were suspended in 2020, it has become a symbol of decline – leaving millions in taxpayer investment unused and communities across Kent and Sussex feeling cut off and forgotten. Reopening the station is about far more than trains. It is a test of whether the Government is serious about backing coastal towns, restoring growth in the South East and tackling the rise of populism in a region where Reform UK is a credible threat. Reinstating international services would turn a ghost station into a driver of good growth, pride and opportunity. It is a visible, economically beneficial step to show that communities on the South East coast are not forgotten – and that the Government is ready to back them. Read More » Sojan Joseph MP 26/08/2025 News My Speech – Mental Health Bill – House of Commons My Speech in the House of Commons on the Third Reading of The Mental Health Bill (2025) [Lords] Read More » Sojan Joseph MP 19/05/2025 News The New Statesman – Spotlight on Policy – Parity of Esteem has Never Been Delivered Our system and attitudes towards mental health are outdated. The fact it has taken 42 years to update the UK’s primary piece of mental health legislation is perhaps the biggest indictment of how reluctant we have been to address it. Read More » Sojan Joseph MP 19/05/2025 News My Speech – Victory in Europe and Victory over Japan: 80th Anniversary – House of Commons Debate I am pleased to have had the opportunity to speak in this debate to recognise the sacrifices made throughout Ashford, Hawkinge and the villages, and to honour the legacy of all those who lived during world war two. We should recognise that it is our duty today to continue to uphold the values they sacrificed so much for Read More » Sojan Joseph MP 06/05/2025 News Kent Live – Fresh calls for Eurostar to return to Kent stops with under-used stations branded a ‘tragedy’ A new campaign urging Eurostar services to return to stopping in Kent has been backed by political voices in the county. pages.text_excerpt: Labour's Missions | Sojan Joseph MP Find me on Facebook 01233 515107 Email Me HOME ABOUT About Me Parliamentary Record NEWS News Articles Newsletter Sign up Newsletters Press Releases Columns ENDORSERS & SUCCESSES Campaigns Missions USEFUL LINKS SURGERIES Coffee Morning HELP Contact Event Request Visit Parliament LABOUR'S MISSIONS Click on the links below to follow some of Labour’s campaigns to make our local area a fairer, greener and safer place for all. Facebook X-twitter Instagram Envelope ECONOMIC GROWTH GET BRITAIN BUILDING AGAIN Good jobs and productivity growth in every part of the country making everyone, not just a few, better off. Details CLEAN ENERGY Great British Energy British people are facing challenges with higher bills. The only way to cut energy bills is to shift to clean, low carbon power. Details NHS & HEALTH Get the NHS back on its feet There when people need it; with fewer lives lost to the biggest killers; in a fairer Britain, where everyone lives well for longer. Details SAFER STREETS Take back our streets Labour believes that feeling secure is the bedrock on which opportunities are built, and communities can thrive. Details OPPORTUNITY barriers to opportunity Reform the childcare and education systems, raise standards everywhere, and prepare young people for work and life. Details HELP US WIN VOLUNTEERING WITH LABOUR Be part of the team that delivers a Labour government. Over 50,000 people have already pledged. pages.title: Surgeries | Sojan Joseph MP
pages.meta_description: I hold face-to-face and online surgeries using Zoom/Microsoft Teams. At each session, I'll be available to help with local issues.
pages.text_excerpt: Surgeries | Sojan Joseph MP Find me on Facebook 01233 515107 Email Me HOME ABOUT About Me Parliamentary Record NEWS News Articles Newsletter Sign up Newsletters Press Releases Columns ENDORSERS & SUCCESSES Campaigns Missions USEFUL LINKS SURGERIES Coffee Morning HELP Contact Event Request Visit Parliament SURGERIES I hold face-to-face and online surgeries using Zoom/Microsoft Teams. At each session, I’ll be available to help with local issues. I hold regular face-to-face surgeries. I also have online advice surgeries via Zoom/Microsoft Teams (by appointment only). Please get in touch with me by email if you can’t get to my surgeries. At the surgeries, I’ll be available to help with various issues, such as education, environmental issues, antisocial behaviour, health and social services, highways, transport, and housing. I can also help constituents on various other issues, such as council tax, bin collections, housing issues, social services, schools and roads and payments, tax and national insurance, benefits, pensions and immigration. 01233 515107 sojan.joseph.mp@parliament.uk Book Appointment: Thursday 7th May 09:30 - 11:30, Central Ashford Virtual Surgery Please type the characters * This helps us prevent spam, thank you.
